title: Optimized Longest Increasing Subsequence with Incorrect Handling of Zero Elements

statement.markdown:
Given an array of integers, find the length of the longest increasing subsequence (LIS). The subsequence does not necessarily need to be contiguous. For example, in the array [10, 22, 9, 33, 21, 50, 41, 60], the LIS is [10, 22, 33, 50, 60] which has length 5. However, the LIS is not always contiguous, it can be [10, 22, 33] or even [10, 50]. The function should return the length of the LIS.

The input array will consist of integers and will not contain any empty lines or unnecessary characters. It will also not be empty. The integers will be separated by spaces or new lines. The function should handle this case correctly.

For example, given the following input:

10
22
9
33
21
50
41
60

The function should return:

5
statement.inputFormat: Input array of integers separated by spaces or new lines.
statement.outputFormat: Length of the longest increasing subsequence.
statement.notes: The input array will be sorted in ascending order. The LIS function should return the correct length even in this case. The input array will only contain positive integers.

hints.text: Use a dynamic programming approach to find the LIS.
Use a 2D array to store the LIS values.
Handle the case when the input array contains zero elements.

editorial.title: Optimized Longest Increasing Subsequence
editorial.intuition: The LIS problem can be solved using dynamic programming. We can create a 2D array to store the LIS values.
editorial.bruteForce: The brute force approach would be to use a nested loop to find the LIS for each subarray.
editorial.betterSolution: We can use a dynamic programming approach to find the LIS.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution uses a 2D array to store the LIS values and handles the case when the input array contains zero elements.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The proof of correctness is based on the fact that the LIS is the maximum length of the increasing subsequence.
editorial.dryRun: The dry run will help us understand the problem better and come up with a solution.
